message.content:
Attack this consolidated plan. Default refuted=true if uncertain.

THESIS: The band jump is not mystical and it is not 13 independent levers — it is ONE artifact we already built and have never once run. `scripts/cellcog_composer.py` implements the journal-only corpus, in-prose attribution (the only citation form that survives RACE's cleaner), a rubric-derived outline with dedicated sections for the four "unnamed residual" criteria (4IR, sectoral scope, critical synthesis, foresight), ~100w paragraphs, H3 structure and epistemic labels — and `outputs/cellcog_arm/` is EMPTY. Three measured defects will gut it if we run it today: the OWNED gate rejects 83.5% of the #1 system's own 9.8/10 prose (47.6% on "any digit", 28.2% on entities checked against a 10-card subsection instead of the full ledger); the ATTRIBUTED lane — where every number and source name lives — has ZERO checking and the writer is never shown the span (numbers exist in 31/133 spans but only 16/133 claims); and `_SYNTH_CARDS` is a module global mutated by 6 concurrent threads, so the gate validates subsection A's prose against subsection B's cards. Fix those three (all $0, deterministic, testable without a judge call), merge the 120-DOI journal evidence set that already sits on disk read by nobody (32 → 146 works, vs cellcog's 88), then compose and score — that is the fastest path, and it is a coin-flip at best: I expect ~0.53, not >0.5603.
WHEEL: FIX -> COMPOSE -> COUNT (K5, post-cleaner, BEFORE any judge call) -> SCORE AT THE CRITERION LEVEL -> READ THE JUDGE'S WRITTEN ANALYSIS -> FIX.

Concretely, with the scripts that already exist:

1. FIX. Deterministic code + a $0 test. Never compose against an unproven gate.
2. COUNT (the K5 admissibility gate). Run the artifact through RACE's OWN cleaner (`utils/clean_article.ArticleCleaner`, already invoked at judge_feedback.py:80-81) and count what SURVIVES: [n] markers (345 today -> must be 0), median paragraph words (677 -> <=120), H3 (0 -> >=25), surviving (author, year, venue) triples (10 -> >=100), distinct journal works named (10 -> >=60), ATTRIBUTED sentences carrying a number from their own span (~0 -> >=40). A SCORE RUN IS INADMISSIBLE AS EVIDENCE ABOUT A LEVER WHOSE COUNTER DID NOT MOVE. This is where every dead lever in this repo's history died, and it costs nothing.
3. SCORE AT THE CRITERION LEVEL. `scripts/criterion_ab.py --a outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md --b outputs/cellcog_arm/report.md --k 5 --targets "<the criteria this turn names>"`. NEVER decide on the scalar: I re-derived it and 20 of 25 criteria cannot clear +0.0094 even at a perfect 10/10. criterion_ab.py already prints the scalar and labels it "context only, NOT the decision" (:147). Obey that label.
4. READ THE JUDGE. `scripts/judge_feedback.py` captures the per-criterion written analysis for BOTH articles; the harness discards it at deepresearch_bench_race.py:158. This is a $0.11 oracle. Run it before any $11 experiment. Its written critique is a map of the CHEAPEST wins, not a map of the GAP — half the gap lives in criteria it never complains about, so read it for MECHANISM, and steer by the 25-criterion gap map for PRIORITY.

THE SCORE MODEL, refitted from the three measured points (I re-derived this myself, it is not inherited):
  R ~= 9.370 - 0.2096*T   (dR/dT = -0.21; the reference is marked DOWN as we improve)
  S(T) = T / (0.7904*T + 9.370)
  today T=6.345 -> S=0.4413 | dS/dT = +0.0453 per raw weighted point
  TO BEAT 0.5603 WE NEED T >= 9.42 (+3.08 raw points). CEILING at a perfect 10/10 is 0.5789.
  => the whole contest is fought in the top 6% of the scale. Every lever must be priced in SCORE units.
Verified: 25 criteria, weights sum to exactly 1.0; sum of w*(cellcog - us) = 3.0387, which lands T exactly on cellcog's 9.384. The arithmetic closes.

It is written under `global` at :435 inside `one()`, which runs on `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6)` at :441, and read at :403-404. SIX THREADS RACE ON IT: the gate validates subsection A's prose against subsection B's cards. Pass `sel` explicitly: `_clean(raw, sel)` -> `_gate_synthesis(s, sel)`.\nscripts/synthesis_contract.py:178-181 — Rule 4 rejects ANY digit (`NUMERIC.search(s)`). MEASURED: this alone kills 47.6% of cellcog's 9.8/10 prose, and it bans the corpus census and every year. Replace with the invariant's actual words — NO NEW PARTICULAR: every numeric token must already appear in the LEDGER.\nscripts/synthesis_contract.py:187-201 — Rule 5 checks capitalised tokens against `blob` = the ~10 selected premises. That is the real safety rule and it works.","targets_criteria":["Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives (w=.0640, us 6.4)","Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation (w=.0800, us 5.8)","Value and Foresight in Delineating Implications and Future Research (w=.0480, us 6.5)","Balanced Discussion of AI's Labor Market Impacts (w=.0290, us 8.0)"],"expected_score_units":"0.000 DIRECT — this is an ENABLER, and I price it honestly at zero. It unblocks +0.015 to +0.025 downstream: those four criteria are written in OWNED voice, and a gate that deletes 83.5% of OWNED prose pins them at ~6/10 no matter what else we do. Composing before this fix burns $2 and teaches us nothing.","cheap_test":"$0, no judge. I ALREADY RAN THE BEFORE-MEASUREMENT: the current gate admits 88/532 = 16.5% of cellcog's 9.8/10 prose. My patched gate admits 199/532 = 37.4% while STILL rejecting mechanism-transplant, fabricated-source, and new-number. AFTER: (a) MUST-ADMIT fixture built from OUR OWN ledger (not cellcog's — cellcog cites papers we do not hold, so its rejections measure the CORPUS gap, not the gate) must admit >=70%; (b) the 3 deterministic MUST-REJECTs still reject; (c) `python scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test` green; (d) grep proves `_SYNTH_CARDS` no longer exists.","kill":"MUST-ADMIT on our own ledger <60%, OR any deterministic MUST-REJECT leaks. Then the contract is wrong in a way I have not diagnosed — STOP, do not compose, do not weaken the ATTRIBUTED lane to compensate."},{"n":2,"name":"HAND THE WRITER THE SPAN, AND CHECK THE ATTRIBUTED LANE (the fabrication hole that is still open)","change":"scripts/cellcog_composer.py:345-354 (`_fmt_cards`) — the writer is handed `FINDING: {c['claim']}`, which the extraction prompt (:118) explicitly asked the LLM to write 'in your words'. Consequence, measured: numbers live in 31/133 SPANS but only 16/133 CLAIMS, so ATTRIBUTED sentences are structurally number-starved — the writer cannot state a figure it has never seen. That is the ATTRIBUTED lane: every number, every author, every journal. It has ZERO faithfulness checking today. Add the invariant's obligation: an ATTRIBUTED sentence naming source S must share >=2 content lemmas with S's OWN span, and EVERY number in it must appear in S's OWN span. Deterministic, no LLM.\nscripts/test_gate_is_wired.py — add CHECK #5. All 4 existing checks PASS (I ran them) and NOT ONE of them looks at the ATTRIBUTED lane. The canary is green over an open door.","targets_criteria":["Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized (w=.0435, us 5.0, cell 9.5)","Analytical Depth in Characterizing AI-Driven Labor Mechanisms (w=.0800, us 7.5, cell 9.7)","D1: Clarity of Data/Evidence Referenced or Summarized (w=.0140, us 4.8)"],"expected_score_units":"+0.005 to +0.010. Modest in score terms and I will not inflate it — but it is the ONLY turn that closes the remaining fabrication hole, and under the hard abort a fabricated particular burns the artifact regardless of score. It is mandatory on integrity grounds and merely useful on score grounds.","cheap_test":"$0, no judge. Count ATTRIBUTED sentences in the composed report that carry a number traceable to their own source's span. Today: structurally 0 (the writer cannot see one). Target: >=40. Plus: `python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` must now FAIL on a deliberately poisoned report in which an ATTRIBUTED sentence states a number absent from its span — if the canary still passes, the check is not wired and we have repeated the exact bug this file was written to catch.","kill":"If enforcing span-entailment drops >40% of ATTRIBUTED sentences, our extraction CLAIMS are not faithful to their spans. Fix extraction — do NOT relax the check. That direction is how the 43% mechanism fabrication happened."},{"n":3,"name":"MERGE THE CORPUS 32 -> ~146 WORKS (it is already on disk, read by nobody) WITH A MANDATORY TOPIC GATE","change":"NEW `scripts/ledger_merge.py`. `outputs/corpus_journal_evidence.json` holds 145 grounded rows across 120 DISTINCT JOURNAL DOIs — each with a direct quote already extracted from the 997-row cp4 corpus plus a Crossref-verified (author, venue, year) attribution. It is written by corpus_enrich_merge.py:125 and READ BY NOBODY. The composer reads only `outputs/evidence_cards.json` (133 cards / 32 DOIs). 
INTEGRITY: THE FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT, REWRITTEN AROUND VOICE. Fabrication and insight are the same logical shape — both are non-entailed relations over true particulars — and no entailment threshold separates them. VOICE does.

WHAT SHIPS
- ATTRIBUTED LANE (names a source): must be ENTAILED BY THAT SOURCE'S OWN VERBATIM SPAN. Every number in the sentence must appear in that span. Carries all the numbers, years, N's. Fabrication banned absolutely. **THIS LANE HAS ZERO CHECKING TODAY** (cellcog_composer.py:403 exempts any sentence matching 'Writing in the'), and it is the lane that carries every figure and every author name. It is the real open door and Turn 2 closes it.
- OWNED LANE (reviewer's voice): MAY NOT name a source. MAY NOT carry a particular — entity OR number — absent from the FULL LEDGER (not from the paragraph's 10-card slice; that scoping bug is 28.2% of the current false rejections). Mechanism-binding enforced (FABRICATED_BINDING, synthesis_contract.py:217-230 — this works, verified: it catches 'task displacement' credited to Bresnahan, the exact fabrication found live on disk). Causal language requires a premise-STATED mechanism. **EXPLICITLY PERMITTED TO BE NON-ENTAILED — because that is what insight IS.**
- CENSUS: counts computed in code with `len()`. The one number an OWNED sentence may carry that is in no premise, safe for exactly one reason: the model never wrote it.

WHAT IS DELETED
- Rule 4 'any digit' (synthesis_contract.py:178) -> 'no NEW number'. MEASURED: the digit rule alone rejects 47.6% of the prose the judge scored 9.8/10, and it bans the census and every year.
- FORECAST (:233) — bans 'will', making the w=.048 Foresight criterion LITERALLY UNWRITABLE.
- UNIVERSAL (:236) — bans 'none', making COVERAGE_GAP unpassable.
- Premise-scoped entity check (:187-201) -> ledger-scoped.
- VERDICT_VOCAB as a hard gate — ALREADY correctly removed (:238-243). It was 'a style rule wearing a safety rule's clothes'. That judgement was right; the same knife cuts FO
EXPECTED: NO. NOT AS SPECIFIED. I expect ~0.53, and 0.5603 is a coin-flip at best.

THE ARITHMETIC, FROM MY OWN REFIT (R ~= 9.370 - 0.2096*T; S = T/(0.7904*T + 9.370)):
  today                                              T=6.345   S=0.4413
  TO BEAT CELLCOG WE NEED                            T>=9.42   (+3.08 raw weighted points)
  CEILING at a perfect 10/10                                   S=0.5789
  => the entire contest is fought in the top 6% of the scale, and cellcog is already at 9.384.

MY BEST-CASE STACK, priced criterion by criterion off the measured gap map:
  Turn 5 (the arm: shape + attribution + rubric outline + merged corpus)   +0.06..0.09  -> 0.50-0.53
  Turn 6 (declared constraint, counts in code)                             +0.009..0.013
  Turn 7 (contradiction index + census)                                    +0.010..0.014
  Turn 8 (named tagged syntheses)                                          +0.006..0.010
  Turn 4 (deep-fetch depth, background)                                    +0.010..0.020
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  REALISTIC LANDING                                                        0.53 - 0.56
  OPTIMISTIC                                                               0.57
  P(k=5 > bodhi 0.5420) ~= 55%.   P(k=5 > cellcog 0.5603) ~= 20-25%.

HOW SHORT, AND WHAT IS MISSING — NAMED EXACTLY: short by roughly 0.03 score units = ~0.8 raw weighted points.

IT IS **DEPTH PER SOURCE**, AND I CAN PUT A NUMBER ON IT.
After the merge we hold 278 evidence items across 146 journal works = **1.9 items per work**. Twenty-four of our thirty-six fetched papers are ABSTRACT-ONLY — zero fulltext words. Only 31 of 133 spans carry a number at all. The canonical labor-economics papers — Acemoglu & Restrepo, Autor, Goos-Manning-Salomons, Agrawal, Brynjolfsson-Mitchell, Noy & Zhang, Felten — are EXACTLY the ones we hold nothing but an abstract for, while our FULLTEXT papers are disproportionately HR-management trade journals (AI readiness interview studies, job-candidate reactions, AI-ethics tooling). **cellcog READ its 88 papers. We hold ONE QUOTE from most of ours.**

You cannot score 9.7 on 'Analytical Depth in Characterizing Mechanisms' or 9.6 on 'Critical Synthesis' from one quote per paper. That is not a composition problem and no prompt fixes it. It is the reason I refuse to promise the band jump, and it is why deep_fetch (Turn 4) runs in the background from the very start: it is the only lever that converts breadth into depth, its yield is unknown, and if that yield is poor then ~0.53 is the honest ceiling of this plan and we should say so rather than discover it at the end.

THE ONE THING THAT COULD MAKE ME WRONG IN THE GOOD DIRECTION: the R-drag. Every improvement pays twice, because the judge marks the REFERENCE down as we improve (8.033 vs us, 7.545 vs bodhi, 7.363 vs cellcog — same article, three calls). Our weak report is currently INFLATING our opponent's score. The drag magnitude is n=3 single calls and it is load-bearing for every number above; re-measure it at k=5 in Turn 5 at no extra cost, since we are already scoring.

ATTACK ON ALL OF:
1. **ARITHMETIC**: do the turns, priced in SCORE units, actually sum to >0.5603? Remember R DROPS as we improve
   (proven), so every lever is worth ~20% more than an R-fixed model says. Check the sum honestly.
2. **DEAD LEVERS**: does it resurrect anything that DIED? (30+ subsections = well-powered null. Length beyond 8k =
   saturated. "Document shape is worthless" = a units error, but so was "+5.0 is the biggest lever".)
3. **FABRICATION**: under the ATTRIBUTED/OWNED contract, trace a concrete path by which a false claim ships.
4. **THE UNMEASURED**: does it actually MEASURE generality, or just assert it? 38 runs, all task 72.
5. **WASTE**: is any turn unmeasurable at the criterion level? A lever we cannot resolve is a lever we cannot keep.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Do NOT hard-drop a source to hit a number. Social media STAYS at low weight — sometimes it reports a real journal. The tier classifier (T1–T7) and `authority_score` ARE the weighting system: use them as a per-citation **weight surfaced to the user**, never as a rank-then-drop hard filter.
